title: rhododendron viscosum f. hispidum
intro: Rhododendron viscosum f. hispidum, also known as sticky azalea, is a deciduous shrub species native to eastern North America, characterized by its sticky or hairy leaves and white to pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Rhododendron viscosum f. hispidum has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
